data_coverage_summary: Epic publishes a large public FHIR catalog with sandbox access for patient, appointment, clinical document, device, observation, medication, procedure, and bulk-data workflows across Epic deployments.
buyer_guidance: Use Epic on FHIR for clinical interoperability, scheduling, patient access, document exchange, and bulk export workflows against Epic-backed organizations. This is an EHR interoperability surface, not an asset or telemetry platform.
overview: Epic's public FHIR site documents a broad resource catalog with sandbox access and client registration for developers building against Epic customer systems. The public resource summary includes read, search, create, update, and operation workflows across Patient, Appointment, AllergyIntolerance, Communication, DocumentReference, Observation, Procedure, ServiceRequest, QuestionnaireResponse, Coverage, Device, DiagnosticReport, and many other clinical resources, along with R4 BulkData kickoff, status, file retrieval, and delete flows. This is a write-capable healthcare interoperability platform, but it does not represent asset or telemetry data. Asset data is Unsupported. Telemetry data is Unsupported. Writeback is Supported.

available_data.category: Patient, scheduling, and clinical resource exchange
available_data.description: Epic on FHIR exposes the clinical and operational resources needed for patient-facing and provider-facing integrations.
data_points: Patient demographics, matching, and summary retrieval
Appointments, appointment search, and scheduling helpers
Allergies, conditions, care plans, care teams, and coverage records
Practitioners, practitioner roles, organizations, locations, and related persons
Medication, procedure, service request, and questionnaire response records

available_data.category: Documents, devices, observations, and bulk export
available_data.description: Epic also documents strong support for document exchange, diagnostic data, and asynchronous population-scale data export.
data_points: Clinical notes, generated CDAs, radiology results, and other document references
Device, device use, and device request records
Vital signs, labs, assessments, social history, and other observations
Diagnostic reports, specimens, and imaging-related metadata
BulkData export status, file retrieval, and delete workflows

ingest_considerations: Epic's public docs are broad, but live integrations remain customer- and app-registration-dependent, so production access is controlled by each Epic-connected organization.

The public site mixes multiple FHIR versions and customer-specific feature areas, so downstream mappings should pin the exact resource flavor and version actually enabled in the target Epic deployment.
Epic exposes strong public human-readable documentation and sandbox support, but no exact public machine-readable OpenAPI or raw CapabilityStatement artifact was confirmed for this aggregate row.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Epic's public resource summary enumerates read, search, create, update, and operation workflows for Patient, Appointment, AllergyIntolerance, Communication, DocumentReference, Observation, Procedure, ServiceRequest, QuestionnaireResponse, Coverage, Device, DiagnosticReport, and R4 BulkData endpoints, with sandbox and client registration available from the public site.
